Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Mary Elizabeth Lemasters For An Order Staying The Action
Off Calendar Aug-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARY LEMASTERS' Motion Of Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Mary Elizabeth Lemasters For An Order Staying The Action.

Granted in part.

Plaintiff brings a legal malpractice case. The underlaying (San Mateo county) case is on appeal, and because that is unresolved, it is not clear what the damages are, or indeed if there will be damages in this malpractice case. Thus plaintiff seeks a stay of this case pending the outcome of the appeal and, perhaps, subsequent trial court proceedings in the underlying case.

The appeal of the underlying case was noticed March 7, 2024, and filed in the First District Court of Appeal. The average time for appeal there is about 466 days, or 1 year, 3.5 months. https://www.courts.ca.gov/documents/2024-Court-Statistics-Report.pdf at pp. 36-37. {Parties may object to my judicial notice of these facts at the hearing on this motion.} So we may reasonably expect a remittitur around July 2025. Unless there is a stay here, the five-year statute in this case will apparently expire July 2027.

The opposition doesn't address any of the authority in favor of stay cited by plaintiff. See motion at 3-4 citing cases such as Beal Bank, SSB v. Arter & Hadden, LLP, 42 Cal. 4th 503, 513 (2007). Instead, the opposition addresses the merits of the underlying case, going so far as to ask for judicial notice of the statement of decision in the underlying case, which is denied as irrelevant.

The only material concern in the opposition is that memories fade. This as stated is a general statement which carries little weight. More specifically, the opposition focuses on the need for plaintiff's deposition and the fact that the agreement in the underlying case was not in writing, thus raising valid concerns that memories of oral agreements and related understandings may fade.

Accordingly the motion for a stay is granted, except that plaintiff's deposition should be promptly taken, and the stay is effective thereafter. The stay expires on the issuance of the remittitur in the underlying case. It is the intent of this order that only plaintiff's deposition is to be undertaken now. As is usual, any party may seek a dissolution of the stay due to e.g., unexpected events including substantial delays in the underlying case.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis C. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SUNENA SABHARWAL, Esq. of Sabharwal Law Offices (Tel: 310-665-8777) for plaintiff;
Atty. RON OAKES-CUNNINGHAM, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendant Morgan, Tidalgo, Sukhodrev and Azzolino, LLP;
Atty. JACKSON L. STOGNER, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for defendant Schoenberg Family Law Group.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MORGAN, TIDALGO, SUKHODREV AND AZZOLINO, LLP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Morgan, Tidalgo, Sukhodrev and Azzolino (MTSA) LLP's demurrer to the complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part.
MTSA demurs as follows: "Plaintiff's Complaint fails [1] to state facts sufficient to establish causation for each causes [sic] of action, as there are not [sic] facts indicating Plaintiff suffered damages from MTSA's alleged conduct. Additionally, Plaintiff has failed [2] to specifically plead misrepresentation with the specificity that California requires. Plaintiff has also failed [3] to plead the terms of the contract that she alleges she entered with MTSA." (Not. 1:25-2:1.)
First, the complaint adequately pleads damage causation. For one example, it pleads that, after MTSA and co-counsel "abandoned" plaintiff, she had to spend money to get another law firm up to speed to defend a summary judgment motion and take a key deposition - services "defendants had already billed" her for. (Cmplt. 6:18-7:5.) Overruled.
Second, plaintiff fails to plead "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) Sustained with leave to amend.
Third, plaintiff adequately pleads the relevant terms of her MTSA contract. (Cmplt. 3:15-22.) Overruled.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SUNENA SABHARWAL, Esq. of Sabharwal Law Offices (Tel: 310-665-8777) for plaintiff;
Atty. RON OAKES-CUNNINGHAM, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendant Morgan, Tidalgo, Sukhodrev and Azzolino, LLP;
Atty. JACKSON L. STOGNER, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for defendant Schoenberg Family Law Group.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MORGAN, TIDALGO, SUKHODREV AND AZZOLINO, LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Morgan, Tidalgo, Sukhodrev and Azzolino (MTSA) LLP's motion to strike addresses emotional distress damages, attorney fees and punitive damages. (Not. 1:23-26.) The motion is granted in part and denied in part.
First, emotional distress damages are unavailable in most negligence cases. But this is more than a negligence case; intentional wrongdoing by attorneys is claimed. (See Smith v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1033, 1040.) Denied.
Second, plaintiff's opposition does not mention - and thus abandons - her prayer for attorney fees. Granted.
Third, punitive damages allegations are adequately pled. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 4:10-5:17; see also Smith, 10 Cal.App.4th at 1041.) Denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
